channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Department of Homeland Security Shuts Down, Though Essential Work Continues
item.description: Though funding for the department ran out early Saturday, officials said its essential functions would continue.

item.title: Pam Bondi’s ‘Cage Match’ and Trump’s Fraying Coalition
item.description: The round table reflects on a chaotic week of incompetence and meltdowns.

item.title: Navalny Was Poisoned With Frog Toxin, European Governments Say
item.description: Traces of the toxin were found in the body of the Russian dissident Aleksei A. Navalny, who died in prison two years ago, five governments said in a j...

item.title: Our K-12 school system is sending us a message: AI tools are for the rich kids
item.description: I worked in Chicago, and saw a world of difference between the North and South Side.

item.title: Private equity’s playbook to shake off the zombies: meet the continuation vehicle
item.description: At one end of the market are the trophy assets, at the other are assets that have yet to meet return targets.

item.title: Your essential services are one surprise failure away from disruption. Consider how physical AI could tackle the crisis
item.description: I'm a physical AI founder and I'm shocked by how our entire economy runs on essential services maintained with methods from the Roman era. Time for ch...

item.title: How Former N.Y.C. Schools Chief Joel Klein Became Friendly With Epstein
item.description: Mr. Klein, who led an education technology company after running the New York City school system, met with Jeffrey Epstein over a period of several mo...

item.title: MacKenzie Scott says her college roommate loaned her $1,000 so she wouldn’t have to drop out—and is now inspiring her to give away billions
item.description: MacKenzie Scott has donated $26 billion in the past several years.

item.title: New U.S. Boat Strike Kills 3 in the Caribbean
item.description: The attacks since early November had specifically targeted suspected drug smuggling boats in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: A widow lost $39,000, her house, and six dogs after a scam. ‘If the story wasn’t so horrible, people wouldn’t pay attention’
item.description: Americans are being defrauded out of billions through an epidemic of romance and investment schemes and it can happen to anybody, authorities warn.

item.title: Live Updates: In Munich, Rubio Calls Europe a Friend but Says It Must Change
item.description: European leaders expressed relief at the tone of Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s remarks, but they made it clear that the trans-Atlantic rift remaine...

item.title: Navalny Was Poisoned With Frog Toxin, European Governments Say
item.description: Traces of the toxin were found in the body of the Russian dissident Aleksei A. Navalny, who died in prison two years ago, five governments said in a j...

item.title: After Mass Shooting, the Town of Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia Is Forever Changed
item.description: The mass shooting in the remote British Columbia mining town has shocked and saddened the nation.

item.title: Roy Medvedev, Soviet Era Historian and Dissident, Is Dead at 100
item.description: His score of books and hundreds of essays documented Stalinist executions, Communist repressions and censorship, and the transition to post-Soviet Rus...

item.title: Zelensky rules out holding elections until there is a cease-fire with Russia.
